info.title: Charge Amps External REST API5
info.description:
Charge Amps external api is intended for integration purposes. Both private and enterprise users can use it. Besides the functionality described below the api provides the following callbacks/web hooks:

  • POST chargepoints/{chargepointid}/boot, returns ChargePoint
  • POST chargepoints/{chargepointid}/heartbeat, returns ChargePointStatus
  • POST chargepoints/{chargepointid}/metervalue, returns MeterValues
  • POST chargepoints/{chargepointid}/connectors/{connectorid}/start, returns ChargingSession
  • POST chargepoints/{chargepointid}/connectors/{connectorid}/stop, returns ChargingSession

CAPI and OCPP, important to note

Two different protocols

  • CAPI, Charge point API, is Charge Amps’ proprietary interface between our cloud and each charger.
  • OCPP, Open Charge Point Protocol, is based on an open standard to enable interoperability between different EV chargers and clouds.

How this affects your API client

  • Both protocols are used within Charge Amps and since some functionalities differ, the API user will need to check which protocol is used for a charger. This in turn tells you how to interpret the charge point status.

To know what protocol is in use, check the property “ocppVersion” of the following endpoints:

  • /chargepoints/{chargepointId}
  • /chargepoints/owned

ocppVersion can have the following values:

  • Value (string) - Description
  • null - The charge point uses the CAPI protocol
  • 1.6 - The charge point uses the OCPP protocol

Changes in version 5 of EAPI:

  • Support for read-only access to chargers, based on organisation setting
  • Extended support for single connector owner on Charge Amps Aura chargers

Note: version 3 is depricated and will be removed in 2022.

Please contact support for more extensive documentation and api key.

post.summary:
Authenticates an enterprise or private user for using the api. A Bearer token is returned if the login is successful. A refresh token
is also returned which can be used to get a new bearer token in case the former one has expired.
Note that an api key also needs to provided in the header.

Gets a new bearer token (and a refresh token) if the former one has expired.
Note, the expired token with the refresh token has to be sent in on order to get new ones
Register new user for external application like mobile

parameters.name: rfidFormat
parameters.description: Required when rfid is supplied and not in hex format

parameters.name: rfidLength
parameters.description: Required when rfid is supplied and not in hex format

parameters.name: rfidDecimalFormatLength
parameters.description: Required when caller wants to pad returned decimal to a specific length

post.summary: Create RFID for partner, not connected to organisation
post.description: RFID hexadecimal is used in the transfer encoding Base16, in which each byte of the plaintext is broken into two 4-bit values and represented by two hexadecimal digits.

get.summary: Get charge point and it's connectors status. Note that measurement is only set if the connector is in charging mode.

put.summary: Unregister chargepoint from user if any

get.summary: Get chargepoint. Expands with settings for charge point and connectors if query parameter expand=settings is applied.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: {settings}

get.summary:
Get chargepoint's charging sessions. All parameters are optional, but in order to limit the number of resulting sessions,
it is recommended that at least one parameter is set.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Optional

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: Optional

parameters.name: maxCount
parameters.description: Maximum number of sessions. Optional

parameters.name: rfidDecimalFormatLength
parameters.description: Required when rfid decimal format should be padded to a specific length

parameters.name: rfidDecimalFormatLength
parameters.description: Required when rfid decimal format should be padded to a specific length

get.summary:
Get connector's charging sessions. All parameters are optional, but in order to limit the number of resulting sessions,
it is recommended that at least one parameter is set.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Optional

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: Optional

parameters.name: maxCount
parameters.description: Maximum number of sessions. Optional

parameters.name: rfidDecimalFormatLength
parameters.description: Required when rfid decimal format should be padded to a specific length

put.summary: Remote start chargepoint. Only makes sense if rifdlock is on.

requestBody.description: rfid needs to be in hexadecimal format (4, 7 or 10 bytes)

put.summary: Remote stop chargepoint. Only makes sense if rifdlock is on.

get.summary: Get chargepoint's schedules. For CAPI chargers only.

put.summary: Update schedule. For CAPI chargers only.

post.summary: Creates schedule. For CAPI chargers only.

get.summary: Get chargepoint's schedule. For CAPI chargers only.

delete.summary: Delete schedule. For CAPI chargers only.

get.summary: Get organisation's charge point and it's connectors status. Note that measurement is only set if the connector is in charging mode.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Optional

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: Optional

parameters.name: rfid
parameters.description: Optional. Rfid used for searching.

parameters.name: rfidFormat
parameters.description: Required when rfid is supplied and not in hex format.

parameters.name: rfidLength
parameters.description: Required when rfid is supplied and not in hex format. Length of tag (4, 7 or 10 bytes).

parameters.name: rfidDecimalFormatLength
parameters.description: Required when caller wants to pad returned decimal to a specific length

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: Optional. Valid values are {rfid}.

parameters.description: Required when caller wants to pad returned decimal to a specific length
